title: centranthus angustifolius var. maroccanus
intro: Centranthus angustifolius var. maroccanus, commonly known as the Moroccan valerian or lesser valerian, is a perennial herb belonging to the valerian family (Valerianaceae) and is native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. Traditionally used in folk medicine, this plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Centranthus angustifolius var. maroccanus include its potential to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its usage and efficacy require further scientific investigation and validation.
